title: Collapsible Object Browser Panel with Persistent State
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want to hide the object selection browser via a toggle, so that I can reclaim screen space after my initial setup is complete.
description:
Add a toggle button labeled "Show Objects" that slides the object selection panel up and down with a smooth CSS animation. The toggle state persists per user in the database so it is remembered across browser sessions.

## Behavior
- Toggle button slides the object selector panel up (hide) or down (show)
- Slide animation provides smooth UX transition
- User's toggle preference is saved to the database
- On page reload, the panel state matches the user's last selection
acceptance_criteria: Toggle button labeled 'Show Objects' is visible on the page
Clicking the toggle slides the object browser panel up or down with animation
Toggle state is persisted in the database per user
Page reload restores the user's last toggle selection
Panel defaults to expanded for new users
ux_changes: New 'Show Objects' toggle button with slide animation to collapse/expand the object selection browser panel.
middleware_changes: Server-side logic to save and retrieve the user's toggle preference.
data_model_changes: New user preference field to store the object browser toggle state.
implementation_notes: Developer proactively added database persistence for the toggle state without being asked, saving the selection per user so it persists across sessions.
comment_summary: Developer implemented the toggle with slide animation and proactively added database persistence for the user's preference. Ryan approved the implementation.
